title: Staff Training & Consultancy | SEND Training for Schools | Psychology Direct
description: CPD-accredited SEND training for teachers and school staff. Bespoke packages delivered by practising Educational Psychologists. Whole-school impact.

blocks.headline: Upskill Your School Staff with Expert SEND Training
blocks.subtitle: CPD-accredited training delivered by practising EPs. Bespoke packages on autism, SEND, behaviour, attachment, and more. Whole-school impact, practical tools, proven outcomes.

blocks.heading: Training & Consultancy Services
blocks.subheading: From one-off staff meetings to term-long consultancy, tailored to your school's needs.

cards.title: Understanding Autism
cards.description: Practical training on autism presentation, sensory needs, communication support, and classroom adaptation. Evidence-based strategies, not assumptions.

cards.title: SEND Support & Early Identification
cards.description: How to identify children with SEND early, what support helps, and how to work collaboratively with families. Reception to secondary.

cards.title: Behaviour Management & Relationships
cards.description: Trauma-informed approaches, attachment-aware practice, and strategies for managing behaviour while maintaining relationships. Whole-school impact.

cards.title: Attachment & Emotional Wellbeing
cards.description: Understanding attachment patterns, supporting children with attachment difficulties, and building emotional safety in classrooms.

cards.title: Supporting Specific Learning Difficulties
cards.description: Dyslexia, dyscalculia, language disorder: how to identify and support in the classroom. Teaching strategies and exam access support.

cards.title: Staff Wellbeing & SENCO Support
cards.description: Supporting staff managing stress and compassion fatigue. SENCO training on strategic SEND leadership and workload management.

cards.title: Bespoke Consultancy
cards.description: Customized training on topics specific to your school's needs. Delivered by experienced EPs familiar with your context.

rows.col1: Bespoke content addressing your school's reality

steps.title: Identify Your Needs
steps.description: Speak with us about what your school needs. Are you training all staff on one topic, or seeking deeper consultancy on a specific challenge?

steps.title: Co-Design Content
steps.description: We work with you to design training that fits your school's context and values. Custom outline, learning outcomes, timings.

steps.title: Delivery
steps.description: An experienced EP delivers the training. Interactive, practical, case-study-based. Teachers leave with tools they can use immediately.

steps.title: Impact & Follow-Up
steps.description: Optional follow-up consultation to embed learning and answer questions. We're available for ongoing support if you choose.

caseStudies.title: Whole-School Autism Approach
caseStudies.description: A secondary academy delivered autism training to all staff. Resulted in better understanding, fewer sensory incidents, and improved experiences for autistic students. Staff reported increased confidence.

caseStudies.title: Behaviour Management Consultancy
caseStudies.description: A primary school implemented trauma-informed behaviour practice through EP consultancy. Exclusions reduced, relationships improved, and staff wellbeing increased.

caseStudies.title: SENCO Leadership Support
caseStudies.description: A SENCO received ongoing consultancy on strategic SEND leadership, workload management, and staff support. Better able to manage LA demands while supporting her team.

faqs.question: What topics can you provide training on?
faqs.answer: We can deliver training on any SEND or educational psychology topic. Common ones include: autism, specific learning difficulties (dyslexia, dyscalculia, ADHD), behaviour management, attachment and emotional wellbeing, communication support, inclusion, early identification. Ask us if your topic isn't listed.

faqs.question: Is the training CPD-accredited?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our courses are eligible for teacher CPD hours. A certificate will be provided specifying the number of hours earned. Teachers can record these toward their annual CPD requirement.

faqs.question: How long does training take?
faqs.answer: Options range from 1-hour staff meeting, to half-day (3-4 hours), full-day (6-7 hours), or multi-session courses (e.g., 6 sessions over a term). We're flexible based on your school's schedule.

faqs.question: How much does training cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on length, topic, and whether it's bespoke or a standard course. Typical: £400-800 for half-day, £700-1,200 for full-day. Multi-session courses priced per school. We always provide a quotation upfront.

faqs.question: Can we customise the training content?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can design bespoke training addressing your school's specific challenges. Initial consultation to understand your needs, then custom content design and delivery.

faqs.question: Who delivers the training?
faqs.answer: An experienced Educational Psychologist with school experience. They understand classroom reality and relate training to your staff's day-to-day work.

faqs.question: Can we involve parents in the training?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some schools deliver training to staff and parents together. Discuss this when you book. We can adapt content for mixed audiences.

faqs.question: What materials do we get?
faqs.answer: Handouts, slide decks, practical resource packs, templates, and follow-up materials relevant to the training topic. Everything designed to be immediately usable in your school.

faqs.question: Do we get follow-up support?
faqs.answer: Follow-up is optional. Some schools book a follow-up consultation 4-6 weeks after training to embed learning and answer questions that have arisen. This often increases impact.

faqs.question: Can your EP come in regularly for ongoing consultancy?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can arrange regular consultancy sessions (monthly, fortnightly, etc.) where your SENCO or leadership team can discuss cases, strategy, and challenges. This has real impact over time.

faqs.question: What if we want to train teachers at specific year groups?
faqs.answer: We can tailor training by phase or year group. EYFS-specific, KS1, KS2, secondary — content is adapted to age-appropriate context.

faqs.question: How do I book training?
faqs.answer: Call 01306 879 975 or email enquiries@psychologydirect.co.uk. Describe what you're hoping to achieve, when you'd like training, and how many staff. We'll send options and a quote.

blocks.heading: Ready to Upskill Your Team?
blocks.bodyText: Quality SEND training transforms how schools support students. Our EPs design bespoke content, deliver with real-world expertise, and provide follow-up support. Let's discuss what your school needs.

blocks.heading: Invest in Your Staff, Transform Your School
blocks.bodyText: Training and consultancy from experienced EPs creates whole-school impact. Upskilled staff, confident children, better outcomes. Let's design a solution for your school.
